engineGuide.h2: Alpha Romeo GT Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Alpha Romeo GT across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: The flagship 1.9 JTDm 150bhp 16v unit, dominant in GT replacement demand for its balance of performance and economy.
compatibleVariants: 1.9 JTDm, 1.9 JTDm 16v, 1.9 JTDm Cloverleaf, 1.9 JTDm Blackline, 1.9 JTDm Euro4
commonFailures: Injector wear - high-pressure injectors degrade from 70k miles causing rough idle.
Swirl flap failure - plastic actuator arms break on pre-2007 units.
DPF clogging - later Euro4 units suffer blockages on short-journey use.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 192B2000 Alpha Romeo GT engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Early 8v 115bhp JTD variant, robust but less powerful than later 16v multijet units.
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging - exhaust recirculation fouls causing performance loss.
Turbo actuator sticking - variable geometry vanes seize with carbon buildup.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 192A5000 Alpha Romeo GT engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Diesel
entries.history: The 20v 175bhp workhorse diesel, favoured for torque and refinement in higher-spec GTs.
commonFailures: Turbo failure - variable geometry mechanism fails causing limp mode.
Crankshaft pulley failure - vibration damper wears causing belt misalignment.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 841G000 Alpha Romeo GT engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Direct-injection JTS petrol engine, introduced advanced technology but prone to carbon issues.
compatibleVariants: 2.0 JTS, 2.0 JTS Distinctive, 2.0 JTS Selespeed, 2.0 JTS Facelift
commonFailures: Carbon build-up - intake valves accumulate deposits causing power loss.
Timing belt failure - interference engine; belt snap destroys valves if missed.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 937A1.000 Alpha Romeo GT engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: Classic Twin Spark petrol unit, reliable when maintained but timing belt critical.
commonFailures: Timing belt failure - interference engine; belt replacement essential every 60k miles.
Oil consumption - valve stem seals wear causing increased oil use on high mileage.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AR32205 Alpha Romeo GT engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2L Petrol
entries.history: Legendary Busso V6, the GT's performance flagship with distinctive sound and character.
commonFailures: Oil consumption - piston rings wear causing increased oil use on high-mileage units.
Timing belt tensioner wear - critical component; failure risks catastrophic engine damage.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 932A.000 Alpha Romeo GT engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Alpha Romeo GT Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Alpha Romeo GT's engine range is generally robust when maintained, but specific failure points drive replacement demand. The 1.9 JTDm diesel and 2.0 JTS petrol dominate enquiries, with timing belt, injector and carbon issues most common. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Why GT Petrol Engines Fail Catastrophically
problems.affectedModels: Alpha Romeo GT 1.8 Twin Spark, 2.0 JTS (2003-2010, AR32205 & 937A1.000)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-90,000 miles if belt not replaced on schedule
problems.rootCause: Rubber timing belt degrades with age and heat; if it snaps, pistons strike valves on these interference engines.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioners, water pump before failure
repairOptions.longevity: 60k miles service interval; only viable before catastrophic damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Restores full reliability; only option if valves/pistons damaged

problems.recommendation: On a GT worth £1,000-£3,000, preventative belt replacement is essential. If the belt has snapped, a rebuilt engine from our network offers better value than dealer repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt JTS engine prices for your Alpha Romeo GT

problems.h4: JTS Carbon Build-up - Why 2.0 Direct Injection Engines Lose Power
problems.rootCause: Direct injection sprays fuel into cylinders, not intake valves; carbon accumulates on valves causing poor airflow.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Walnut blasting or chemical cleaning of intake valves and manifold
repairOptions.longevity: Restores power temporarily; may need repeating every 30k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt JTS engine with updated components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution if carbon damage has compromised valve seats

problems.recommendation: Carbon cleaning is cost-effective if caught early. If power loss is severe or valves are damaged, a rebuilt JTS engine offers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt 937A1.000 Alpha Romeo GT engine

problems.h4: JTD Injector Failure - Why 1.9 Diesel Engines Run Rough
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 70,000-120,000 miles depending on fuel quality and maintenance
problems.rootCause: High-pressure common-rail injectors wear internally, causing poor spray pattern, misfires and increased emissions.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty injectors, code to ECU, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Restores smooth running; other injectors may fail later

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 1.9 JTDm engine with upgraded injectors, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if multiple injectors failed or engine has high mileage

problems.recommendation: Single injector replacement makes sense on lower-mileage GTs. If multiple injectors have failed or the engine exceeds 120k miles, a rebuilt unit is more economical.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 192B2000 engine prices for your Alpha Romeo GT

problems.h4: JTD Swirl Flap Failure - Why Diesel GTs Lose Performance
problems.rootCause: Plastic swirl flap actuator arms become brittle and break; fragments can enter cylinders causing catastrophic damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove flaps, blank manifold, remap ECU to clear fault codes
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix for flap issue; may affect emissions compliance

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt JTD engine if fragments entered cylinders, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option if engine suffered mechanical damage from flap debris

problems.recommendation: If no mechanical damage occurred, swirl flap blanking is the most reliable fix. If fragments entered the engine, replacement is the only safe option.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt JTD engine for your Alpha Romeo GT

problems.h4: Turbo Failure - Why GT Diesels Enter Limp Mode
problems.affectedModels: Alpha Romeo GT 1.9 JTDm, 2.4 JTD (2003-2010, all JTD codes)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 80,000-150,000 miles depending on oil change intervals
problems.rootCause: Variable geometry turbo vanes stick with carbon; actuator fails causing boost loss and limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbocharger, new gaskets, oil feed line, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost; ensure oil system is clean to protect new turbo

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt JTD engine if turbo failure caused oil contamination damage
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine suffered secondary damage from turbo failure

problems.recommendation: Turbo replacement is cost-effective if the engine is otherwise healthy. If oil contamination damaged bearings, a rebuilt engine offers better long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt diesel engine prices for your Alpha Romeo GT

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Alpha Romeo GT
finalCta.paragraph: The Alpha Romeo GT is a driver's car worth preserving. Whether you're facing timing belt worries, JTS carbon issues, or JTD injector failure, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Alpha Romeo GT Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Alpha Romeo GT and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Alpha Romeo GT Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Alpha Romeo GT is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

groups.title: Alpha Romeo GT Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Alpha Romeo GT 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.9L diesel was the GT's volume seller, offered in 8v and 16v multijet forms from launch through facelift. Its balance of economy and torque made it the most searched replacement option, though injector and swirl flap issues drive many enquiries.
commonFailurePoints: swirl flap actuator failure on pre-2007 units
items.cta: Get quotes for Alpha Romeo GT 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Alpha Romeo GT 2.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.4L JTD 20v offered enhanced refinement and torque for higher-spec GTs, particularly the Cloverleaf. Less common than the 1.9 but sought after for its smoother delivery, though turbo and EGR issues feature in replacement searches.
items.cta: Get quotes for Alpha Romeo GT 2.4L diesel engines

groups.title: Alpha Romeo GT Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Alpha Romeo GT 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level 1.8 Twin Spark petrol offered classic Alfa character with dual spark plugs per cylinder. Reliable when maintained but timing belt critical; popular with budget-conscious GT owners seeking affordable replacement options.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure if not replaced every 60k miles
items.cta: Compare Alpha Romeo GT 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Alpha Romeo GT 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0 JTS direct-injection petrol was the GT's technological flagship, offering strong performance but prone to carbon build-up on intake valves. Dominates petrol replacement searches due to both popularity and known issues.
commonFailurePoints: timing belt critical on interference design
items.cta: Compare Alpha Romeo GT 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Alpha Romeo GT 3.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The legendary 3.2L Busso V6 powered the GT's performance variants, delivering distinctive sound and 240hp. Low-volume but high-value replacement searches from enthusiasts prioritising provenance and specialist rebuild quality.
items.cta: Compare Alpha Romeo GT 3.2L petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Alpha Romeo GT Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the GT's 2003-2010 production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The GT launched with a focused engine lineup: 1.8 TS petrol, 2.0 JTS direct injection, and 1.9/2.4 JTD diesels.
keyChanges: GT introduced on 156-derived platform with unique coupe body
Initial engine range: 1.8 TS, 2.0 JTS, 1.9 JTD 8v/16v, 2.4 JTD 20v
Selespeed automated manual offered on 2.0 JTS variants
knownFor: Launch enthusiasm for GT's styling and driving dynamics
Early JTD swirl flap concerns on pre-2007 units
JTS carbon build-up begins appearing in owner reports
years.cta: Get quotes for 2003-2005 Alpha Romeo GT replacement engines

faq.h2: Alpha Romeo GT Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace an Alpha Romeo GT engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Alpha Romeo GT engines typically cost £800-£2,000 for 1.9 JTDm diesels and £1,000-£2,000 for 2.0 JTS petrols, supply only. Supply & fit adds £400-£900 labour depending on location and garage.
keyPoints: 1.9 JTDm rebuilt engines average £800-£1,500 supply only
2.0 JTS rebuilt units range £1,000-£2,000 depending on damage
Labour for supply & fit typically adds £400-£900 nationwide
Prices vary by engine code, variant and extent of damage
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Alpha Romeo GT engine prices for your variant

items.question: Is the Alpha Romeo GT engine worth replacing?
items.answer: Yes, if your GT is in good condition otherwise. Replacement engines from £800 make economic sense versus scrapping a £1,000-£3,000 car, especially with our 12-month warranty coverage.
keyPoints: GT values range £1,000-£4,000 depending on variant and condition
Rebuilt engines from £800 offer strong value versus replacement car cost
12-month unlimited mileage warranty protects your investment
Enthusiast models like 3.2 V6 Cloverleaf justify higher rebuild spend
items.cta: Get quotes to see if replacement makes financial sense for your GT

items.question: What are the most common Alpha Romeo GT engine problems?
items.answer: Timing belt failure on petrol units, carbon build-up on 2.0 JTS, and injector/swirl flap issues on 1.9 JTDm diesels dominate GT replacement enquiries. Regular maintenance prevents most catastrophic failures.
keyPoints: Petrol GTs: timing belt critical every 60k miles on interference engines
2.0 JTS: carbon build-up on intake valves causes power loss
1.9 JTDm: injector wear and swirl flap failure common from 70k miles
2.4 JTD: turbo VGT actuator and EGR valve issues feature in searches
items.cta: Compare prices for engines addressing your GT's specific failure

items.question: Will a 156 engine fit my Alpha Romeo GT?
items.answer: Some 156 engines share codes with the GT (like 192B2000 JTDm) but ECU, immobiliser and exhaust differences often prevent direct compatibility. Always verify engine code and variant before purchasing.
keyPoints: Engine codes may match but wiring looms and ECUs often differ
Immobiliser systems require matching ECU or professional recoding
Exhaust routing and mounts can differ between 156 and GT platforms
Use our registration check to confirm exact compatible codes for your GT
items.cta: Get quotes for verified GT-compatible engines only

items.question: What engine codes does the Alpha Romeo GT 1.9 JTDm use?
items.answer: The GT 1.9 JTDm uses codes 192B2000 (150bhp 16v), 192A5000 (115bhp 8v), and 192B5000 (Euro4 DPF variant). The 192B2000 is the most searched replacement engine for this model.
keyPoints: 192B2000: 150bhp 16v multijet, most common GT diesel replacement
192A5000: 115bhp 8v early unit, robust but less powerful
192B5000: Euro4 DPF-equipped variant, facelift models only
Always match code exactly to avoid ECU and emissions issues
items.cta: Compare prices for your exact 1.9 JTDm engine code

items.question: How long does an Alpha Romeo GT engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines typically dispatch within 1-3 working days from UK specialists. Full supply & fit service usually completes in 2-4 days depending on garage workload and parts availability.
keyPoints: Supply-only: 1-3 day dispatch from verified UK suppliers
Supply & fit: 2-4 days typical including removal, fitting, road test
Complex variants like 3.2 V6 may require specialist scheduling
Urgent replacement options available; request expedited quotes
items.cta: Get quotes with your preferred turnaround timeframe

items.question: Do you supply and fit Alpha Romeo GT engines nationwide?
items.answer: Yes, our network includes vetted UK specialists offering supply & fit service nationwide. Request quotes with fitting included to compare total costs from garages in your region.
keyPoints: 100+ vetted UK specialists in EngineMarket network
Supply & fit quotes include engine, labour, fluids and road test
Nationwide coverage from Scotland to South West England
Single warranty contact simplifies aftercare versus separate supplier/fitter
items.cta: Find Alpha Romeo GT supply & fit deals in your area

items.question: Are reconditioned Alpha Romeo GT engines reliable?
items.answer: Yes, when sourced from reputable rebuilders. Reconditioned GT engines undergo inspection, wear-part replacement and testing; our network suppliers provide minimum 12-month warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Reconditioning includes cleaning, measurement and worn-part replacement
Reputable rebuilders address known GT failure points during process
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard through EngineMarket
Request rebuild specification details to confirm scope of work
items.cta: Compare reconditioned Alpha Romeo GT engine options with warranty

items.question: What warranty comes with Alpha Romeo GT replacement engines?
items.answer: Every engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from the supplying specialist. Confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across network
Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing/rebuild defects
Supply & fit warranties often include fitting labour at original garage
Always confirm warranty scope and claims process before purchase
items.cta: Get quotes with warranty terms clearly stated for your GT

items.question: Should I repair or replace my Alpha Romeo GT engine?
items.answer: Replace if damage is catastrophic (timing belt snap, swirl flap ingestion) or the engine exceeds 120k miles. Repair may suit minor issues like single injector failure on lower-mileage GTs.
keyPoints: Catastrophic damage (belt snap, flap ingestion) warrants full replacement
High-mileage engines (>120k) often better replaced than repaired
Single-component failures (one injector) may justify targeted repair
Compare repair quotes versus rebuilt engine prices before deciding
items.cta: Compare repair versus replacement costs for your specific GT issue
